cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about a search group formed by American scientists and what they did. The options are A to D. First, I need to refer back to the passage. The relevant part is in the 1975 paragraph. It says, "In 1975, however, some American scientists formed a search group. They used an underwater camera. It took pictures every seventy seconds. Some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature. Its body was about four meters long and had a very ugly head on the end of a four meter neck. Many people then began to believe in the monster. But even today we can not be certain." So the key points here are that the American scientists used an underwater camera that took pictures every 70 seconds. Some of those pictures seemed to show a monster-like creature. But they didn't actually find the monster itself, just images that looked like a monster. Also, the passage mentions that they used a camera, so the action was taking pictures.
